title: SKILL.md Restructure + Agent Productivity Optimization
description: Two-phase overhaul: (1) Restructure SKILL.md monolith (1390 lines / 75KB) into routing map + modular docs — enabler for everything else. (2) Optimize agent orchestration: thin Alex, named subagents, parallel execution, token optimization. CC-native first, SDK deferred.

in: Split SKILL.md into routing map (<150 lines) + docs/pipeline/*.md + docs/reference/*.md
Create named subagent definitions (auditor, builder, reviewer) with scoped tools
Build deterministic shell scripts for mechanical tasks (classify, read-context, checkpoint)
Enable parallel execution for brainstorm, challenge, and independent initiatives
Token optimization via tool scoping and model routing across all agents
Update Alex spawn prompt to reference routing map instead of inlining full SKILL.md
out: Changing pipeline logic or step ordering

dod.criterion: SKILL.md is <150 lines with only routing logic + file references
Every step exists in exactly one docs/pipeline/*.md file
Zero content loss — concatenated docs match original
Named subagent definitions exist with scoped tools
Deterministic scripts handle classification, context, checkpoints
Brainstorm + challenge phases spawn agents in parallel
50%+ reduction in per-agent spawn token overhead

tasks.title: Extract pipeline step docs from SKILL.md
tasks.scope: Analyze SKILL.md and identify natural split boundaries. Extract into 10 docs/pipeline/ files. Steps 0a+0b MUST stay grouped (triage drives routing), Step 5 splits into 3 docs (execution-phases, review-protocol, execution-lifecycle). Checkpoint Protocol → reference docs.
definition_of_done: 10 pipeline docs exist under docs/pipeline/ covering all steps
Steps 0a+0b grouped in one doc
Step 5 split into 3 docs
Each doc has verification marker comment at top
Each doc self-contained for its execution phase

tasks.title: Extract reference docs (schemas, templates, rules) from SKILL.md
tasks.scope: Extract 7 schemas, 5 templates, 4 rules files into docs/reference/ subdirs. Morgan's prompt casting rules + phase definitions become shared reference docs used by both planning and execution pipeline docs.
definition_of_done: 7 schema files in docs/reference/schemas/
5 template files in docs/reference/templates/
4 rules files in docs/reference/rules/
Morgan's casting rules extracted as shared reference
Reviewer prompts consolidated into single template

tasks.title: Build SKILL.md routing map and update Alex spawn prompt
tasks.scope: Replace SKILL.md from Step 0b onward with routing table (step ID, purpose, file path, deps). Update Step 0a spawn template from ~68KB inline → ~500 byte reference. alex-cos.md unchanged.
definition_of_done: SKILL.md is <150 lines
Routing table covers every pipeline doc + dependencies
Step 0a spawn template references routing map
Each routing entry includes verification marker
'How to Use This Map' section for Alex

tasks.title: Verify zero content loss across the restructure
tasks.scope: Write verification script checking 58 headings, 7 JSON schemas, ~40 code blocks. Distinguish real headings from template headings inside code blocks. Run and fix any gaps.
definition_of_done: Verification script passes with zero MISSING/DUPLICATE headings
Word count delta <5% between original and concatenated docs
All JSON schemas accounted for

tasks.title: Create Role-Specific Subagent Definitions
tasks.scope: Create auditor.md, builder.md, reviewer.md in .claude/agents/ with scoped tools, appropriate model, and minimal 2-3KB prompts
definition_of_done: Three new agent files exist at .claude/agents/auditor.md, builder.md, reviewer.md
Each file has tools: field restricting to role-appropriate tools only
Each file has model: field (haiku for auditor, inherit for builder/reviewer)
Each system prompt is under 3KB
Prompts include role-specific instructions

tasks.title: Create Deterministic Shell Scripts
tasks.scope: Create classify-directive.sh, read-context.sh, checkpoint.sh in scripts/ for weight classification, context assembly, and checkpoint CRUD
definition_of_done: Three executable scripts exist in scripts/
classify-directive.sh accepts directive name, outputs JSON with weight
read-context.sh accepts role parameter, outputs assembled context
checkpoint.sh supports init/update-step/update-initiative/read subcommands
All scripts have usage documentation in comments

tasks.title: Slim Alex's Personality + Add Tools Restriction
tasks.scope: Add tools: restriction to alex-cos.md (no Write/Edit), trim personality to coordination-only, update modular pipeline docs to reference new role agents
definition_of_done: alex-cos.md has tools: field excluding Write and Edit
alex-cos.md is under 5KB
Pipeline docs reference subagent_type: auditor/builder/reviewer for generic work

tasks.title: Parallel Brainstorm Spawning
tasks.scope: Update pipeline docs for brainstorm and challenge phases to use run_in_background: true, add collection pattern and error handling
definition_of_done: Brainstorm phase includes run_in_background: true in spawn templates
Challenge phase includes run_in_background: true
Collection pattern documented after each parallel spawn section
Error handling for failed background agents documented

tasks.title: Parallel Initiative Execution
tasks.scope: Update execution pipeline docs for parallel initiative execution based on non-overlapping active_files, add parallelism analysis, update current.json schema, document pattern
definition_of_done: Execution pipeline doc includes parallelism analysis logic
Non-overlapping initiatives spawn with run_in_background: true
Error recovery documented for failed parallel initiatives
lessons/orchestration.md updated with parallel execution pattern

tasks.title: Add Tools and Model Routing to All Existing Agents
tasks.scope: Add tools: field to all 10 existing agent definitions with role-appropriate restrictions. C-suite gets read-only, specialists get write tools. Biggest single token savings (~60% reduction).
definition_of_done: All 10 agent definitions have tools: field with role-appropriate restrictions
C-suite agents restricted from Write/Edit unless role requires it
Priya retains WebSearch/WebFetch
Sam (QA) has Bash but no Write/Edit
Token reduction documented
